question: Is the image rendered in a realistic style with crystal clear seawater?
Does the image appear to be a wide-angle shot?
Are there exactly 2 divers positioned in the left foreground?
Are both divers swimming horizontally toward the right?
Is the front diver wearing a black wetsuit with a lime green tank?
Is the rear diver wearing a bright pink wetsuit with a yellow tank?
Is the rear diver positioned further away and slightly out of focus (depth of field)?
Are there exactly 2 black underwater cameras (one in front of each diver)?
Does the front camera have a transparent waterproof housing with the lens pointing lower right?
Is the rear camera's lens pointing toward the center of the frame?
Are there 2 streams of bubbles varying in size rising from the divers?
Do the bubble streams drift toward the upper right?
Are there exactly 2 sea turtles positioned in the center of the frame?
Are the sea turtles swimming from right to left?
Does the front turtle have a light purple shell with pale markings?
Does the rear turtle have an olive green shell?
Is there visible distance maintained between the two turtles (not overlapping heavily)?
Are there exactly 3 angelfish positioned below the turtles?
Are the angelfish blue and yellow with semi-transparent fins?
Are the angelfish swimming to the right in a small formation?
Are there exactly 7 clownfish positioned to the lower left of the turtles?
Are the clownfish surrounding a sea anemone?
Are there exactly 3 classic orange and white striped clownfish?
Are there exactly 2 darker blackish clownfish?
Are there exactly 2 smaller juvenile orange and white striped clownfish?
Are the 7 clownfish positioned at staggered heights?
Are there exactly 2 starfish in the lower right corner on sandy bottom?
Is there one common red starfish and one pure white starfish?
Do both starfish have five complete arms?
Are there exactly 2 small crabs positioned to the left of the starfish?
Do the crabs have dark brown carapaces and antennae pointing forward?
Are the crabs peeking out from coral crevices?
Is there a cluster of red branching coral extending upward with rounded tips at the bottom center?
Is there a cluster of spherical purple brain coral with grooves to the right of the red coral?
Are there exactly 2 sea anemones positioned above the purple brain coral?
Is the left anemone (above brain coral) light green and the right one bright yellow?
Is there a patch of dark green seagrass in the lower left bending right with the current?
Is there a gray stingray in the right middle area?
Is the stingray gliding close to the seabed with a long thin tail pointing right?
Is there shipwreck wooden plank debris in the distant upper left background?
Are the divers' bodies free from severe anatomical distortions (e.g., extra fingers, twisted limbs)?
Are there any severe anatomical errors on the marine creatures (e.g., extra fins, deformed shapes)?
Is the image free of heavy blur, extreme noise, or rendering glitches that obscure key objects?
